task_completion.visual_evidence: The main menu or interface of the GCompris educational application is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'Simplified Tower of Hanoi' activity
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Simplified Tower of Hanoi' activity is open, displaying three vertical pegs.

task_completion.description: Verify the first level starts with 4 discs on the left peg
task_completion.visual_evidence: Exactly 4 discs of varying sizes are stacked on the leftmost peg, with the largest at the bottom and the smallest at the top. The middle and right pegs are empty.

task_completion.description: Move discs following the rules (one at a time, no larger disc on a smaller disc)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Intermediate screenshots show discs distributed among the three pegs. In all visible stacks, smaller discs are always placed on top of larger discs, never the reverse.

task_completion.description: Move all 4 discs to the rightmost peg to solve the level
task_completion.visual_evidence: All 4 discs are successfully stacked on the rightmost peg (largest at the bottom, smallest at the top). The left and middle pegs are completely empty. A success animation or level completion indicator may be visible.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GCompris GUI to move the discs
integrity.visual_evidence: The mouse cursor is seen dragging and dropping the discs between pegs within the GCompris application interface, rather than using terminal commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent is playing the actual GCompris 'Simplified Tower of Hanoi' activity
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window matches the standard GCompris interface graphics, icons, and layout for the Hanoi activity, and is not a different web-based Hanoi game or mock-up.

integrity.description: The game state transitions logically without cheating or glitches
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of multiple discs moving simultaneously, discs floating without being dragged by the cursor, or discs teleporting. The sequence of screenshots shows a logical progression of single-disc moves.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The GCompris activity is named 'Simplified Tower of Hanoi' (internal name 'hanoi') and is found in the 'discovery logic' category. The first level features exactly 4 discs. The minimum number of moves to solve this level is 15. The expected final state is all 4 discs stacked on the rightmost peg, with the largest at the bottom and the smallest at the top.
